_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites 'text' from live numbers; these are the computed fallbacks so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.cape: The Shiller CAPE ratio stands at 40.7 in June 2026, more than double its 140-year average of 17.3 and only slightly below the record high of 44.19 set in December 1999, signaling extreme market overvaluation by historical standards.
notes.buffett: The Buffett Indicator, which compares total market capitalization to GDP, has surged to 231.7% in June 2026—far exceeding the dot-com peak of 146% and 2007's peak of 110%—highlighting an unprecedented disconnect between stock prices and economic output.
notes.concentration: Market concentration is unusually high, with the top 5 companies comprising nearly 30% of the S&P 500, the tech sector making up 48% of the index, and the top 10 stocks accounting for 41% in 2026 compared to just 17.5% in 2015, increasing systemic risk tied to a few dominant players.
notes.yieldcurve: The yield curve spread between 10-year and 3-month Treasuries is a modest 0.55 points, while the New York Fed assigns a 31% chance of recession, reflecting market concerns about future economic downturn despite a currently positive slope.
notes.drypowder: Berkshire Hathaway holds a record $397.4 billion in cash as of Q1 2026, indicating significant dry powder, yet 79% of institutions expect a decline in liquid assets, suggesting reduced buying power amid potential market stress.
notes.finale: Between December 2026 and May 2027, IPO lock-up expirations for major companies like SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic will release large shares into the market, potentially draining liquidity when combined with shrinking institutional cash reserves and heightening market volatility.
